behavior_summary: This script implements an egg hatching sequence. When the egg's U8P_FISHEGG::hatch trigger fires, it checks an item-local guard (Egg::getEggId) and, if not set, marks the egg as started (Egg::setEggId = 1) and spawns an asynchronous worker that runs a timed, randomized hatching animation which creates transient visual pieces, plays SFX at key frames, and destroys those pieces when their animation finishes.
A leave-fast-area handler clears the egg's guard (Egg::setEggId = 0) so the egg can be reinitialized later.

The asynchronous worker repeatedly: schedules cleanup via a spawned helper, suspends/yields between steps, uses urandom(100) to pick between two visual branches, creates an item fragment (Item::create), positions it at the egg's coordinates (Item::getX/Y/Z + Item::pop), advances its visual frames (Item::setFrame), optionally plays SFX at particular frames, and destroys the fragment when its animation completes.
notable_control_flow: Guard check in U8P_FISHEGG::FISHEGG::hatch: only spawn the worker if Egg::getEggId(this) == 0 — prevents concurrent/duplicate hatching workers.
Spawner behavior: U8P_FISHEGG::FISHEGG::hatch sets Egg::setEggId(this, 1) then spawns U8P_FISHEGG::FISHEGG::00CC (as asynchronous process) and stores the spawn return value locally.

Worker loop in U8P_FISHEGG::FISHEGG::00CC: each iteration spawns FREE::1C19 tied to the worker's pid (cleanup), yields/suspends, branches on urandom(100) > 50 to select one of two fragment/item types and frame sequences, creates/populates the item at egg coordinates, advances frames over multiple iterations with SFX at thresholds, and destroys the fragment when its sequence is finished.
open_questions: The exact event binding: U8P_FISHEGG::hatch is inferred to be a fast-area entry trigger (Event 7) but the precise trigger type (enter vs other) is not fully proven.
Concrete item type IDs (the specific item kinds created during the animation) and exact SFX IDs used are not listed in the disassembly output and remain unknown.
Precise timing/delay values used by the worker between frames and the lifetime (frame counts) of created fragments are inferred from counters but exact numeric timing is unspecified.
The exact semantics and implementation details of FREE::1C19 (spawned repeatedly) are inferred as a cleanup helper but its precise role and parameters remain uncertain.
Whether Egg::setEggId is used elsewhere in game logic (beyond guarding concurrent workers) is not known from this file alone.

main_function.name: U8P_FISHEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Handle the egg 'U8P_FISHEGG::hatch' trigger: if the egg is not already initialized (eggId == 0), mark it started and spawn an asynchronous worker to run the hatching animation/sequence.
entry_conditions: Event 7 (U8P_FISHEGG::hatch) — invoked when the egg's U8P_FISHEGG::hatch trigger fires (inferred fast-area/entry-type event).
Egg::getEggId(this) == 0 must hold for the worker to be spawned.

function_map.name: U8P_FISHEGG::hatch
function_map.role: Entry handler for the egg's U8P_FISHEGG::hatch trigger; guards and spawns the asynchronous U8P_FISHEGG::hatch worker.

function_map.name: U8P_FISHEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Handler for leave-fast-area that clears the egg's id/flag (Egg::setEggId(this, 0)) so the egg can be restarted later.

function_map.name: U8P_FISHEGG::00CC
function_map.role: Asynchronous U8P_FISHEGG::hatch worker process: runs the timed/randomized animation, creates transient fragments, plays SFX, and destroys fragments when finished.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn the asynchronous U8P_FISHEGG::hatch worker/process after setting the egg guard (Egg::setEggId = 1).

external_calls.purpose: Read the egg's stored id/flag to determine whether a U8P_FISHEGG::hatch worker is already running (guard).
Set the egg's stored id/flag to 1 to mark that hatching has been started (prevents duplicate workers).
Clear/reset the egg's id/flag to 0 when the actor leaves the trigger area so the egg can be reinitialized.
Spawn a cleanup/delayed-free helper tied to the worker's pid; used repeatedly to schedule cleanup (inferred).
Generate a 0..99 random value (urandom(100)) to choose between two animation/item branches (~50% split).
Create a temporary visual fragment/hatchling item used in the animation; the specific type depends on the random branch.
Obtain the egg's X coordinate to position the spawned fragment.
Obtain the egg's Y coordinate to position the spawned fragment.
Obtain the egg's Z coordinate to position the spawned fragment correctly in elevation.
Place/insert the newly created fragment item at the egg's coordinates with an initial frame/orientation.
Advance the fragment's visual frame/state to animate cracking/hatching progression.
Play sound effects at particular frame thresholds to emphasize cracking/U8P_FISHEGG::hatch events.
Destroy/remove the temporary fragment when its animation sequence completes.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements an egg hatching sequence. When the egg's U8P_FISHEGG::hatch trigger fires, it checks an item-local guard (Egg::getEggId) and, if not set, marks the egg as started (Egg::setEggId = 1) and spawns an asynchronous worker that runs a timed, randomized hatching animation which creates transient visual pieces, plays SFX at key frames, and destroys those pieces when their animation finishes.
